title: MT Executor orchestrator integrating job queue + mailbox + loop control + backpressure
goal: Land the top-level `MicroTaskExecutor` orchestrator that ties X.1 mailbox primitives + X.2 job queue + loop control + cancellation + scheduler + outcome recording into a single executor loop. This is the entrypoint a worker session calls to execute MTs end-to-end with all preservation-slice invariants enforced.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/mt_executor.rs with `MicroTaskExecutor::run(executor_identity, config) -> impl Future`; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/mt_executor_integration_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: All X.1 and X.2 primitives merged so the executor orchestrator has every dependency available.
implementation_notes: Top-level loop: `loop { (1) check cancellation token; (2) call FairScheduler::next_for_executor (MT-187); (3) if Some(job) -> claim via MicroTaskQueue (MT-184); (4) acquire mailbox lease for job.mailbox_thread_id via LeaseManager (MT-180) with router permission check (MT-181); (5) load MT contract from job.mt_contract_path; (6) for each iteration up to MtLoopControlBudget (MT-185): (a) record_checkpoint; (b) check backpressure for outgoing mailbox messages (MT-182); (c) execute the MT body (delegates to a Coder trait — coder impl is out of scope here, MT executor receives a `MtCoderHandle: trait` provided by caller); (d) parse completion_signal; (e) if Verification needed -> post micro_task_verification_needed message to mailbox (MT-179) and await; (f) if iteration succeeds -> record outcome (MT-188) Success branch; (g) if iteration fails -> record outcome (MT-188) Failure branch which routes escalation; (h) check cancellation token between every iteration boundary (MT-186); }`. After completion, post `micro_task_completion_report` mailbox message (MT-179) and release lease (MT-180). Errors at any step: emit FR-EVT-MT-EXEC-ERROR with structured cause, record outcome=Failure, do NOT panic. The MtCoderHandle trait surface: `trait MtCoderHandle { async fn execute(&self, ctx: MtExecutionContext) -> Result<MtIterationResult, MtCoderError>; }` — keeps the coder impl pluggable so cluster C can wire LlamaCppRuntime/CandleRuntime adapters and tests can use a stub. Integration tests: (a) end-to-end Happy Path — stub coder returns Success on iteration 1, verify outcome + mailbox completion message + lease released; (b) Failure path — stub returns Failure twice, verify escalation tier moves T7B->T7BAlt, mailbox escalation message posted; (c) Cancellation path — request cancellation mid-iteration, verify Cancelled state + cleanup; (d) Backpressure path — flood mailbox to saturate, verify Deny propagated as a soft retry not a hard failure; (e) Lease lost mid-iteration (simulated expiry) — verify graceful re-acquire or Abandon outcome.
risk_if_missed: Without the orchestrator, all X.1/X.2 primitives are unwired libraries; no executor session can actually run an MT through the preservation-slice contracts; the cluster X fold delivers zero end-to-end behavior.
validator_focus: Orchestrator never panics — every error path records outcome=Failure with structured cause; cancellation observed within bounded latency; lease re-acquire path proven (does not silently double-claim).

note: Queue primitive and schema tests passed.

implementation_or_hardening_notes: model_session_scheduler_tests now use a live Postgres-only harness with isolated schemas and focused schema installation; no SQLite harness path is used.
Focused schema includes ai_job_mcp_fields so session observability spans can persist MCP/tool-call correlation rows.

Model-run dispatch locking is per SessionRegistry rather than a process-global async lock, reducing cross-test contention while preserving deterministic dispatch within a registry.
Session worktree add/remove operations are serialized around git worktree metadata.

minimum_controls: End-to-end test asserts zero panics across 5 happy/failure/cancellation paths.
Lease re-acquire after simulated expiry asserts router permission re-check (not stale token reuse).
MtCoderHandle trait is sealed or marker-trait-protected so unauthorized coders cannot be substituted at runtime.

implementation_summary: Added contract-path process_ledger::mt_executor orchestrator with MicroTaskExecutor::run(executor_identity, MtExecutorRunConfig).

Wired scheduler claim, queue state transitions, mailbox lease/router acquisition, backpressure-checked typed mailbox posts, loop checkpoints, outcome persistence, cancellation re-check after awaited coder work, FR-EVT-MT-EXEC-ERROR emission hook, and marker-protected AuthorizedMtCoder.
Retained the legacy mt_executor::executor::run_job surface for existing tests while making the durable orchestration path explicit.

out_of_contract_touch_requires_operator_.why: MT-189 owned a new process_ledger/mt_executor.rs module; Rust compilation required registering the module in the parent mod.rs.

sources_checked: LangGraph durable execution / checkpoints documentation: checkpoints persist state within and across interactions, with Postgres checkpointer support.
Temporal durable execution documentation: crash-proof workflows resume where they left off after process or infrastructure failure.
Google ADK sessions documentation: sessions carry id, history/events, state, and append_event lifecycle for continuity.
Agent2Agent task documentation: tasks are stateful entities with lifecycle states, optional history, and artifacts.
selected_patterns: Checkpoint before/around execution boundaries instead of relying on chat chronology.
Treat task/job state, history, artifacts, cancellation, and failure as durable first-class data.
Use explicit executor identity and routing/lease decisions before side-effecting mailbox writes.
Return structured deferred/failed/cancelled outcomes instead of panicking on infrastructure failures.
rejected_options: Extending only the legacy run_job single-job loop; rejected because it cannot claim from the queue or coordinate mailbox leases.

Replacing the public MtCoderHandle trait with a sealed trait; rejected because it would break existing integration-test stubs and downstream adapters. Selected marker-protected AuthorizedMtCoder for the durable run path instead.
Running the literal packet cargo command as final proof; rejected as too broad for this crate layout after it compiled unrelated test binaries until the 20-minute timeout.
validation_plan: Adjacent MT executor, queue, scheduler, outcome, mailbox lease/router/backpressure test binaries.
Direct rustfmt check on touched files.
Postgres end-to-end test remains ignored until POSTGRES_TEST_URL is available.

postgres_test_url_orchestrator_smoke_202.summary: Live POSTGRES_TEST_URL pass implemented/fixed the residual Postgres paths: MT-189 durable executor smoke, Locus GetMtProgress parity on Postgres, atomic scheduler claim to prevent duplicate SKIP LOCKED claims, checkpoint timestamp precision normalization, and raw_sql schema application for multi-statement Postgres migrations.
product_changes: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs implements LocusOperation::GetMtProgress with the same structured progress metadata projection as SQLite.
src/backend/handshake_core/src/mt_executor/scheduler.rs now claims with one atomic UPDATE ... FROM candidate ... RETURNING job_id statement guarded by FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED and state=queued.
src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/mt_loop_control.rs normalizes checkpoint created_at_utc to Postgres microsecond precision at creation time.
src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox_v1/repo.rs and src/backend/handshake_core/src/mt_executor/scheduler.rs use sqlx::raw_sql for multi-statement schema setup.
src/backend/handshake_core/tests/observability_span_repo_tests.rs uses sqlx::raw_sql for multi-statement migration setup.

Pre-existing warnings remain in unrelated code paths and were not addressed in this session.
